name: Slurm Preemption And Requeue
summary: Handle Slurm preemption signals and guarded requeue workflows.
description: This skill helps users make Slurm jobs signal-aware, checkpoint-friendly, and safe to restart when preemption, node failure, or an explicit requeue starts the batch script again with the same job id.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Only needed for operator-reviewed manual requeue actions.

inputs.name: checkpoint_dir
inputs.description: Durable user-owned directory where progress state is written before termination or requeue.

inputs.name: signal_policy
inputs.description: Slurm signal, warning time, and site preemption policy to review before enabling the wrapper.

inputs.name: restartable_command
inputs.description: Application or workflow command that can resume from checkpoint state without corrupting prior output.

outputs.name: checkpoint_state
outputs.description: Step marker or application checkpoint files that make a restarted batch script idempotent.

outputs.name: signal_log
outputs.description: Evidence showing whether the job saw a warning signal, stop request, or restart path.

outputs.name: requeue_review
outputs.description: Operator checklist for deciding whether an explicit manual requeue is appropriate.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax without submitting jobs.
Submit on a short test partition after replacing placeholders and confirm a repeated run resumes from existing state.
